name: markdown-link-title
description: Paste a URL in Markdown and get [Page Title](url). Zero dependencies, honours VS Code proxy settings.

markdownLinkTitle.enabled.description: Master on/off switch for converting pasted URLs into Markdown links.

markdownLinkTitle.fetchTitle.description: Fetch the page title for the pasted URL. When off, behaves like the native paste (inserts an empty label for you to type).

markdownLinkTitle.timeoutMs.description: Timeout in milliseconds for the title fetch before falling back to an empty label.

markdownLinkTitle.preferOpenGraph.description: Prefer the og:title meta tag over the <title> element when both are present.

markdownLinkTitle.maxRedirects.description: Maximum number of HTTP redirects to follow while resolving a title.

markdownLinkTitle.userAgent.markdownDescription: User-Agent header sent with the title fetch. Leave empty to use the default link-preview bot (`Twitterbot/1.0`), which many sites whitelist to return clean `og:title` metadata where a browser User-Agent hits a bot-wall.

markdownLinkTitle.placeholder.description: Temporary label shown in the link while the title is fetched in the background, e.g. [fetching…](url).

markdownLinkTitle.maxBodyKb.description: Maximum amount of the response body to read, in kilobytes, while looking for the title.

markdownLinkTitle.maxTitleLength.description: Maximum length of a fetched title before it is truncated at a word boundary with an ellipsis. 0 disables truncation.

enumDescriptions: Insert a numbered reference [title][1] and append [1]: url at the end of the file.
markdownLinkTitle.linkStyle.description: How to insert the link. 'reference' uses Markdown reference-style links with the URLs collected at the bottom of the file.
